title: Lagenocypsela latifolia
taxonomy: Lagenocypsela latifolia belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. It is a genus of flowering plants native to Australia. The classification of Lagenocypsela latifolia also includes it being part of the order Asterales, and its kingdom being Plantae. Within the family Asteraceae, Lagenocypsela latifolia is further grouped into the subfamily Asteroideae. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup.

intro: Lagenocypsela latifolia, also known as the broad-leaved Lagenocypsela, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
